title: Crust Plugin Wire Protocol
description: JSON Schema for the crust plugin wire protocol. Plugins are external processes that communicate with the crust engine over newline-delimited JSON on stdin/stdout.

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: A filesystem or network operation type.

severity.title: Severity
severity.description: Severity level. Invalid values default to "high" at runtime.

action.title: Action
action.description: What to do when a rule matches. Empty or omitted defaults to "block" at runtime.

ruleSnapshot.title: RuleSnapshot
ruleSnapshot.description: A read-only, JSON-safe view of an engine rule. Plugins can use this for context-aware decisions (e.g., checking if a path is already protected by a builtin rule).

priority.description: Rule priority. Lower values = higher priority. Default is 50.

block_except.description: Exception glob patterns (allowed even if block_paths matches).

message.description: Block message shown to the user.

enabled.description: True if the rule is currently active.

hit_count.description: Number of times this rule has matched.

initParams.title: InitParams
initParams.description: Parameters sent with method="init". Contains the plugin name and optional plugin-specific configuration.

name.description: Plugin name, used for logging and identification.

config.description: Plugin-specific configuration. Structure depends on the plugin. null means use defaults.

evaluateRequest.title: EvaluateRequest
evaluateRequest.description: Parameters sent with method="evaluate". Contains everything the engine extracted during steps 0-12, plus a snapshot of all active rules.

arguments.description: Raw JSON arguments from the tool call. May be null for tools with no arguments.

operation.description: Primary operation detected for this tool call.

operations.description: All operations detected (a command may both read and write).

command.description: Raw shell command (Bash tool only, empty for other tools).

content.description: Write content or full raw JSON of all arguments.

evasive.description: True if the command uses shell tricks that prevent static analysis.

rules.description: Read-only snapshot of all active engine rules at evaluation time.

evaluateResult.title: EvaluateResult
evaluateResult.description: Result of an evaluate call. null means allow; an object means block.

oneOf.title: Allow
oneOf.description: null means the plugin allows the tool call.

oneOf.title: Block
oneOf.description: Non-null result means the plugin is blocking the tool call.

plugin.description: Auto-filled by the registry with the plugin's name. Plugins should not set this.

severity.description: Severity level. Invalid or missing values default to "high" at runtime.

action.description: What action to take. Empty or omitted defaults to "block" at runtime.

wireRequest.title: WireRequest
wireRequest.description: A JSON-RPC-like request sent from crust to the plugin process. One JSON object per line on stdin.

oneOf.title: Init Request
oneOf.description: One-time initialization with plugin name and config.

oneOf.title: Evaluate Request
oneOf.description: Evaluate a tool call that passed all built-in checks.

oneOf.title: Close Request
oneOf.description: Graceful shutdown. Plugin should respond with {"result":"ok"} and exit.

wireResponse.title: WireResponse
wireResponse.description: A JSON-RPC-like response sent from the plugin process to crust. One JSON object per line on stdout. Exactly one of "result" or "error" must be present.

oneOf.title: Success Response
oneOf.description: Successful response. The result value depends on the method: "ok" for init/close, null or EvaluateResult for evaluate.

result.description: For init/close: the string "ok". For evaluate: null (allow) or an EvaluateResult object (block).

oneOf.title: Error Response
oneOf.description: Error response. The plugin encountered an error processing the request.

title: Wire Protocol Message
description: A single message in the crust plugin wire protocol. This schema covers both directions: WireRequest (crust to plugin) and WireResponse (plugin to crust).
